rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving type errors and invalid references in implementation-level contracts.
Ensure that implementation-level guarantees only reference valid outputs or assignments within the implementation scope.
generic_rules: When AGREE reports type mismatches, align helper declarations with the correct scalar types: int, real, or bool.
If a guarantee references an unresolved identifier, remove or rewrite the clause to use only visible AADL features.
Move implementation-specific bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes when needed.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the input AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must resolve to valid types; use only declared helpers or visible ports in guarantees.
Implementation-level contracts should reference outputs or assignments that are valid in the implementation scope.
anti_patterns: Do not add new identifiers or types to AGREE contracts that are not already present in the AADL model.
Do not attempt to fix unresolved references by inventing new declarations; instead, remove or rewrite the clause.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs. implementations
Model Analyst agent's role in extracting AADL structure
